hypothesis: The v4k tied token embedding and lm_head should remain an on-chip board-facing continuation because rowwise int8 tied vocab plus position storage is only 63 BRAM36 by the memory scorecard and the existing output-head top1 RTL already maps at 64 BRAM36 with 4 DSP lanes. The useful next gate is therefore not another synthesis pass, but a deterministic tied-storage replay that proves one physical row pack can serve both embedding lookup and streamed top-k output-head comparison.

storage_layout.physical_table: single tied token embedding/lm_head row store

row_format.sidecar_role: fixed-point scale metadata for converting int32 dot products into a comparable logit domain

storage_layout.layout_note: The 64-BRAM output-head RTL packs only the token table for compute. The memory scorecard 63-BRAM value is the compact theoretical unique rowwise int8 token-plus-position storage ceiling and should be reconciled by the next replay/package script.

embedding_lookup.consumer: token embedding plus position embedding add before layer 0

prototype_contract.recommended_package_shape: v4k on-chip tied-vocab table with shared row pack for embedding lookup and streamed lm_head top-k

determinism.tie_break: lower token id wins for equal fixed-point scores

board_status.hardware_block_reason: no board access in this run

next_replay_gate.reason: Python and apply_patch are unavailable; creating a non-executable script would not improve evidence
inputs: v4k residual-add int8 output vector from h2-v4k-int8-l2-mlp-chain-residual-add-rtl-proof
row pack emitted for the output-head RTL
required_checks: same physical row bytes decode for embedding lookup and lm_head row dot product
top1 and top5 agreement against f32 logits for at least 8 deterministic prompts once multi-sample traces exist
fixed-point top-k tie break is stable
packed token table byte count is 278528 before banking padding
banked RTL memory padding is explained separately from logical tied-storage bytes

decision.rationale: The current evidence already supports v4k on-chip tied vocab as the next board-facing surface; the missing evidence is shared row-pack replay over more than one sample, not another no-board build.
decision.reject: Do not start v4k board replay, OpenFPGALoader, JTAG, FTDI/MPSSE polling, or full TinyStories synthesis in this no-board run.
decision.next_gate: Recover Python/apply_patch, then implement the tied row-pack top-k replay script and regenerate this artifact from source weights and trace artifacts.
